content.problemStatement:
Given two strings A and B of the same length Output "Yes" if B can be obtained by rearranging the characters in A, otherwise output "No".


Input Format

The first line contains the integer: NNN - the size of the 2 strings
The second line contains two strings AAA and BBB.


Output Format
Output "Yes" or "No".
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 100000
A and B contain only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- A line with an integer N, the length of the two strings.
- A line with two space‑separated strings A and B, each of length N consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if B can be obtained by rearranging the characters of A, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Both strings contain exactly the same multiset of characters, therefore B is an anagram of A.
The character 'e' appears in A but not in B, while 'f' appears in B but not in A, so they are not anagrams.
The strings have the same characters with the same frequencies, just in different order.

solution.approach: For each test case we count the occurrences of each of the 26 lowercase letters in both strings. If the two frequency arrays are identical, the strings are anagrams and we output "Yes"; otherwise we output "No". Counting is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra space (26 counters). An alternative is to sort both strings and compare, which is O(N log N), but counting is optimal.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the strings. Overall O(sum of N) ≤ 1e5.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        a = next(it)
        b = next(it)
        cnt = [0] * 26
        for ch in a:
            cnt[ord(ch) - 97] += 1
        for ch in b:
            cnt[ord(ch) - 97] -= 1
        out.append('Yes' if all(x == 0 for x in cnt) else 'No')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
